  • Tools: tl

Tools: tl#

The tools namespace contains model-fitting, gene extraction, regulator inference, and network-loading functions.

Drift analysis#

scjdo.tl.fit_drift(adata, *, time_key='pseudotime', rep='X_pca', hidden=256, depth=4, beta=0.1, use_spectral_norm=True, vel_scale=2.0, vel_time_mode='flat', n_epochs=5000, batch_size=512, lr=0.0002, weight_decay=0.0001, sigma=0.1, use_local_sigma=False, seed=42, device=None, n_windows=100, overlap=0.8, smooth_sigma=1.5, n_archetypes=5, n_restarts=5, key_added='scjdo', verbose=True)[source]#

Train a drift field and compute archetype decomposition.

Automatically derives a pseudotime-gradient velocity prior when no RNA velocity is available, so the drift field follows the developmental path.

Results are stored in adata.uns[key_added]:

Key

Content

J_tensor

Jacobian tensor (n_windows, D, D), numpy

t_centers

Window pseudotime centers (n_windows,), numpy

patterns

Archetype patterns (K, D, D), numpy

activations

Temporal activations (n_windows, K), numpy

max_real_eig

Max real eigenvalue per window (n_windows,)

gene_scores

Gene loadings per archetype {k: (n_genes,)}

corr_mat

Pairwise activation correlations (K, K)

params

Training configuration dict
Parameters:

  • adata (AnnData after sjd.pp.prepare_trajectory.)

  • time_key (Column in adata.obs with normalized pseudotime [0,1].)

  • rep (Key in adata.obsm for the latent representation.)

  • n_epochs (Training iterations.)

  • n_archetypes (Number of operator archetypes (K).)

  • vel_scale (Strength of the pseudotime-gradient velocity prior.)

  • vel_time_mode (Gate shape — 'flat' (constant), 'root', 'rise', 'mid'.)

  • key_added (Where to store results in adata.uns.)

  • hidden (int)

  • depth (int)

  • beta (float)

  • use_spectral_norm (bool)

  • batch_size (int)

  • lr (float)

  • weight_decay (float)

  • sigma (float)

  • use_local_sigma (bool)

  • seed (int)

  • device (str | None)

  • n_windows (int)

  • overlap (float)

  • smooth_sigma (float)

  • n_restarts (int)

  • verbose (bool)

Examples

>>> sjd.tl.fit_drift(adata, time_key="pseudotime", n_archetypes=5, n_epochs=5000)
>>> adata.uns["scjdo"].keys()
dict_keys(['J_tensor', 't_centers', 'patterns', 'activations', ...])
scjdo.tl.get_instability_genes(adata, key='scjdo', n_genes=20, activation_threshold=0.5, sensitivity_threshold=0.05, min_sensitive_fraction=0.0, top_archetypes=None)[source]#

Extract the top genes driving instability from each archetype, ranked by how much the archetype is associated with locally sensitive (positive-eigenvalue) pseudotime windows.

An archetype is characterised as “instable” when it is strongly active (activation > activation_threshold) during windows where the maximum real eigenvalue exceeds sensitivity_threshold.

Parameters:

  • n_genes (Top N genes to return per archetype.)

  • activation_threshold (Minimum normalised activation to consider a) – window “active” for that archetype.

  • sensitivity_threshold (Minimum Re(λ_max) to consider a window sensitive.)

  • min_sensitive_fraction (Minimum fraction of active windows that must be) – sensitive to include the archetype (0 = all).

  • top_archetypes (If set, return only the K most instable archetypes.)

  • key (str)

Returns:

archetype, mean_sensitivity, sensitive_fraction, peak_pseudotime, gene, instability_score, rank

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame with columns

Schrödinger Bridge analysis#

scjdo.tl.fit_bridge(adata, *, time_key='pseudotime', rep='X_pca', src_quantile=0.2, tgt_quantile=0.8, src_group=None, tgt_group=None, groupby=None, hidden=256, depth=4, epsilon=0.5, n_score_steps=500, max_iterations=30, n_archetypes=4, t_steps=30, n_traj=80, steps=100, n_genes=20, sens_thresh=0.05, act_thresh=0.5, seed=42, device=None, key_added='scjdo_bridge', verbose=True)[source]#

Full Schrödinger Bridge pipeline: train, simulate, compute instability archetypes, and extract top instability genes for both forward and backward directions.

All results are stored in adata.uns[key_added].

Population selection#

By pseudotime quantile (default):

sjd.tl.fit_bridge(adata, src_quantile=0.20, tgt_quantile=0.80)

By cell-type label:

sjd.tl.fit_bridge(adata, groupby='cell_type',
                  src_group='progenitor', tgt_group='committed')
param src_quantile / tgt_quantile:

source / target populations.

type src_quantile / tgt_quantile:

Bottom / top pseudotime fraction to use as

param src_group / tgt_group / groupby:

cluster label rather than pseudotime.

type src_group / tgt_group / groupby:

Alternative: define populations by

param n_archetypes:

type n_archetypes:

Number of operator archetypes per bridge direction.

param t_steps:

type t_steps:

Bridge time steps for Jacobian analysis.

param n_genes:

type n_genes:

Top instability genes to extract per archetype.

returns:

bridge – adata.uns[key_added]['_bridge']).

rtype:

Trained SchrodingerBridge object (also stored in

Parameters:

  • time_key (str)

  • rep (str)

  • src_quantile (float)

  • tgt_quantile (float)

  • src_group (str | None)

  • tgt_group (str | None)

  • groupby (str | None)

  • hidden (int)

  • depth (int)

  • epsilon (float)

  • n_score_steps (int)

  • max_iterations (int)

  • n_archetypes (int)

  • t_steps (int)

  • n_traj (int)

  • steps (int)

  • n_genes (int)

  • sens_thresh (float)

  • act_thresh (float)

  • seed (int)

  • device (str | None)

  • key_added (str)

  • verbose (bool)

scjdo.tl.get_bridge_instability_genes(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', n_genes=None, top_archetypes=None)[source]#

Return ranked instability gene tables for forward and backward bridge directions.

Parameters:

  • n_genes (Limit to top N genes per archetype (None = all stored).)

  • top_archetypes (Return only the K most sensitive archetypes per direction.)

  • key (str)

Returns:

df_fwd, df_bwd – archetype, peak_t, mean_sensitivity, gene, instability_score, rank

Return type:

pandas DataFrames with columns

Regulatory inference#

scjdo.tl.infer_regulators(adata, key='scjdo', direction='forward', network=None, network_source='auto', organism='mouse', min_targets=3, n_top=20, compute_pseudotime_lead=False, denovo_n_top=15, key_added='scjdo_regulators', verbose=True)[source]#

Infer upstream TF regulators from instability-associated genes.

Scores each TF on six metrics (weighted out-degree, mean target instability, regulon enrichment, branch specificity, database confidence, pseudotime lead) and infers de novo co-instability edges from Jacobian eigenvector structure.

Works with both sjd.tl.fit_drift and sjd.tl.fit_bridge results.

Parameters:

  • key (adata.uns key from fit_drift (‘scjdo’) or fit_bridge (‘scjdo_bridge’).)

  • direction (For bridge — 'forward', 'backward', or 'both'.)

  • network (Custom TF-target DataFrame [source, target, weight].)

  • organism ('mouse' or 'human'.)

  • min_targets (Min shared targets to include a TF.)

  • n_top (Return top N regulators.)

  • compute_pseudotime_lead (Compute pseudotime lead-lag (slower).)

  • denovo_n_top (Genes per window for de novo edge inference.)

  • key_added (Key to store results in adata.uns.)

  • network_source (str)

  • verbose (bool)

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame — ranked regulators.

Examples

>>> sjd.tl.fit_drift(adata)
>>> df = sjd.tl.infer_regulators(adata)
>>> sjd.pl.regulator_summary(adata)

>>> sjd.tl.fit_bridge(adata)
>>> df = sjd.tl.infer_regulators(adata, key='scjdo_bridge',
...                               direction='both')
scjdo.tl.load_network(organism='mouse', source='auto', custom=None)[source]#

Load a signed TF-target regulatory network.

Priority: CollecTRI (decoupler) → TRRUST v2 (web) → built-in hematopoiesis.

Parameters:

  • organism ('mouse' or 'human'.)

  • source ('auto' | 'collectri' | 'trrust' | 'builtin'.)

  • custom (User-supplied DataFrame [source, target, weight].)

Return type:

DataFrame

Stored drift results#

Key

Shape

Description

J_tensor

(T, D, D)

Smoothed Jacobian tensor

t_centers

(T,)

Window pseudotime centers

patterns

(K, D, D)

Archetype Jacobian patterns

activations

(T, K)

Non-negative temporal activations

max_real_eig

(T,)

Maximum real eigenvalue per window

instability_scores

(T, n_genes)

Gene-level instability scores

top_instability_genes

list

Globally ranked instability genes

Regulator scoring metrics#

Metric

Output column

Interpretation

Weighted out-degree

weighted_score

Total instability explained by TF targets

Mean target instability

mean_instability

Quality of target instability signal

Regulon enrichment

enrichment_score

Hypergeometric enrichment score

Branch specificity

branch_specificity

Archetype preference on a 0–1 scale

Database confidence

db_confidence

Mean confidence of source-network edges

Pseudotime lead

pseudotime_lead

Optional TF-before-target timing score

previous
